dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3841 IS: Last Sale Valuation Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cassidy (for himself and Mr. Whitehouse) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Tariff Act of 1930 to modify the determination of transaction value for customs valuation.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Last Sale Valuation Act of 2026.

section.header: Modification to determination of transaction value for customs valuation
section.text: Section 402 of the Tariff Act of 1930 (19 U.S.C. 1401a) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (b)(4), by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The term sold for exportation to the United States, with respect to imported merchandise, means—

clause.text: in the case of a transaction involving one sale, the price actually paid or payable by the buyer in the United States to the seller located in another country for the merchandise; and

in the case of a transaction involving a series of sales, the price actually paid or payable by the buyer in the United States for the merchandise in the last sale that introduces the merchandise into the United States.

paragraph.text: in subsection (h)(5), in the flush text following subparagraph (C), by inserting , including access to books and records by U.S. Customs and Border Protection after adjustment.
